summary.overall_regression: Critical regression: health score 80 → 60 (+20 pts). 2 critical incident(s).
summary.causal_chain_text: Isolated anomalous events (no confirmed causal chain): 'db file sequential read' (57.1% DB time)
summary.headline: DB Time rose 24% driven by: bottleneck shift CPU→I/O + 13 new application SQL(s) (top: 31uyb0jgyzmuf, 97s).
headline_evidence: 1 SQL(s) regressed without plan change (+541s cumulative)
Top worsened wait: 'db file sequential read' 28.5%→57.1% DB Time

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
pathology_investigate: missing indexes causing unselective index scans

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Parallel query or serial large table scan bypassing buffer cache. Common with parallel_degree_policy=AUTO.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Parallel query or serial large table scan bypassing buffer cache. Common with parallel_degree_policy=AUTO.
pathology_investigate: large table scans in top SQL

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Multi-block read — full table or index range scan. db_file_multiblock_read_count blocks per I/O.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Multi-block read — full table or index range scan. db_file_multiblock_read_count blocks per I/O.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Hot block contention — multiple sessions reading/writing the same buffer simultaneously.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Hot block contention — multiple sessions reading/writing the same buffer simultaneously.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.
pathology_investigate: top segments by buffer busy waits

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Reading sort/hash results from TEMP tablespace — PGA insufficient, operation spilled to disk.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Reading sort/hash results from TEMP tablespace — PGA insufficient, operation spilled to disk.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Writing sort/hash results to TEMP. Same root cause as direct path read temp.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Writing sort/hash results to TEMP. Same root cause as direct path read temp.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.
pathology_investigate: large result sets in top SQL (rows/exec)

sql_regressions.net_assessment_detail: SQL not present in bad period

recommendations.finding: 2 critical SQL statement(s) present in the GOOD period are completely absent from the BAD period AWR (top: 96abn4q2fw1mn, 7uq4jru8y1n3c). These accounted for 443s of elapsed time in the GOOD period. This is strong evidence the batch job or process did NOT complete its primary processing work — it stalled, failed, or was blocked before reaching these execution stages. The lower DB Time in the BAD period reflects less productive work, NOT lower load.
recommendations.action: 1. Check application/batch job logs for errors or early exits during the BAD period. 2. Verify the job reached its processing stages (scheduler logs, job history). 3. Identify what WAS running in BAD that prevented these SQLs from executing. 4. Compare the execution plans of SQLs that ran in BAD vs GOOD for divergence signs.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: 4 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL 7tv22qfsdmt4r — 138s → 678s (+109%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
recommendations.action: Use Real-Time SQL Monitoring for execution-level visibility. Compare plans: SELECT * FROM TABLE(DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR('<sql_id>')); Gather fresh statistics or use SQL Plan Baseline to lock good plan.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'db file sequential read' classified as worsening: 312.9s -> 780.2s (+6%) [Both volume and latency increased]
recommendations.action: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'db file sequential read';

incident_indicators.description: 2 critical SQL statement(s) present in the GOOD period are completely absent from the BAD period AWR (top: 96abn4q2fw1mn, 7uq4jru8y1n3c). These accounted for 443s of elapsed time in the GOOD period. This is strong evidence the batch job or process did NOT complete its primary processing work — it stalled, failed, or was blocked before reaching these execution stages. The lower DB Time in the BAD period reflects less productive work, NOT lower load.
incident_indicators.remediation: 1. Check application/batch job logs for errors or early exits during the BAD period. 2. Verify the job reached its processing stages (scheduler logs, job history). 3. Identify what WAS running in BAD that prevented these SQLs from executing. 4. Compare the execution plans of SQLs that ran in BAD vs GOOD for divergence signs.
Check DBA_HIST_SQL_PLAN for plan changes. Use DBMS_SPM to lock good plans.

incident_indicators.description: 4 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL 7tv22qfsdmt4r — 138s → 678s (+109%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
incident_indicators.remediation: Use Real-Time SQL Monitoring for execution-level visibility. Compare plans: SELECT * FROM TABLE(DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR('<sql_id>')); Gather fresh statistics or use SQL Plan Baseline to lock good plan.
incident_indicators.diagnostic_sql: SELECT sql_id, plan_hash_value, executions, elapsed_time/1e6 AS elapsed_s, cpu_time/1e6 AS cpu_s FROM dba_hist_sqlstat WHERE sql_id = '7tv22qfsdmt4r' ORDER BY snap_id DESC;

incident_indicators.description: 'db file sequential read' is a 3.0σ statistical outlier: 57.1% of DB time (baseline mean: 7.2%). This event is anomalously dominant in the bad period.
incident_indicators.remediation: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.

sql_plan_changes.sql_text_full: delete from LANEEXCEPTION /* InvOpt.RIO.PurgeLaneExceptionQueryLivehint */ WHERE ( EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM PROCESSLANE WHERE PROCESSLANE.source = LANEEXCEPTION.SOURCE AND PROCESSLANE.DEST = LANEEXCEPTION.DEST AND PROCESSLANE.TRANSMODE = LANEEXCEPTION.TRANSMODE AND processid = :1 AND batchnum = :2 AND ordergroup = ' ') OR (ordergroup IN (SELECT ordergroup FROM processlane WHERE processid = :3 AND BATCHNUM = :4 AND ordergroup <> ' '))) AND ( EXCEPTIONID >= 13000 AND EXCEPTIONID <= 13010 )

all_metrics.label: DB Time/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Total database work per second. Core workload intensity metric.

all_metrics.label: DB CPU/sec
all_metrics.explanation: CPU consumed by database per second. Increasing means more compute demand.

all_metrics.label: Logical Reads/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer gets per second. High values indicate intensive SQL scanning.

all_metrics.label: Physical Reads/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Disk read blocks per second. High value with low Read IO Requests/s = large multi-block full-table scans (direct-path, invisible to Segments section). High value with proportionally high Read IO Requests/s = many single-block index reads. Compare both metrics together.

all_metrics.label: Physical Writes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Disk writes per second. Increase may indicate more DML or redo/undo activity.

all_metrics.label: Redo Size/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Bytes of redo generated per second. Higher means more DML workload.

all_metrics.label: Hard Parses/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Hard parses per second. >100/s indicates literal SQL or cursor invalidation.

all_metrics.label: Executes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: SQL executions per second. Application throughput indicator.

all_metrics.label: Transactions/sec
all_metrics.explanation: User commits+rollbacks per second. Primary business throughput KPI.

all_metrics.label: Logons/sec
all_metrics.explanation: New session creations per second. Spike signals connection pool churn.

all_metrics.label: User Calls/sec
all_metrics.explanation: User-originated calls per second. Overall application request rate.

all_metrics.label: Block Changes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer blocks modified per second. Correlates with redo and DML volume.

all_metrics.label: Buffer Cache Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: Data found in RAM vs disk. <95% = excessive physical I/O.

all_metrics.label: Library Cache Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: SQL look-ups that found the cursor in memory. Low = shared pool too small.

all_metrics.label: Soft Parse %
all_metrics.explanation: SQL executions that reused a cached plan. <95% = hard parse storm.

all_metrics.label: Execute to Parse %
all_metrics.explanation: % executes reusing open cursor. Low = cursor caching not enabled.

all_metrics.label: Latch Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: Internal Oracle lock efficiency. <99% = contention in shared memory.

all_metrics.label: DB Time (min)
all_metrics.explanation: Total database active time. Core load indicator.

all_metrics.label: Avg Active Sessions
all_metrics.explanation: Sessions actively working. >CPU count = saturation.

all_metrics.label: Transactions/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Business throughput KPI (commits+rollbacks per second).

all_metrics.label: Elapsed Window
all_metrics.explanation: Observation window length. Large difference = unequal comparison.

all_metrics.label: db file sequential read
all_metrics.explanation: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.

all_metrics.label: log file sequential read
all_metrics.explanation: Wait event contributing 0.3% of DB time.

all_metrics.label: latch: cache buffers chains
all_metrics.explanation: Hot block contention — multiple sessions reading/writing the same buffer simultaneously.

all_metrics.label: buffer busy waits
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.

insights.title: 4 New Wait Event Bottlenecks
insights.summary: These wait events appeared only in the bad period — they're entirely new contention points.
evidence: log file sequential read: 3.6s (0.3% DB time)
latch: cache buffers chains: 3.0s (0.2% DB time)
insights.root_cause: New contention from new SQL, changed concurrency, or infrastructure issues.
insights.action: Match each new wait event to the SQL or session causing it via ASH data.

insights.title: Incident: Critical Sql Disappeared
insights.summary: 2 critical SQL statement(s) present in the GOOD period are completely absent from the BAD period AWR (top: 96abn4q2fw1mn, 7uq4jru8y1n3c). These accounted for 443s of elapsed time in the GOOD period. This is strong evidence the batch job or process did NOT complete its primary processing work — it stalled, failed, or was blocked before reaching these execution stages. The lower DB Time in the BAD period reflects less productive work, NOT lower load.
insights.root_cause: 2 critical SQL statement(s) present in the GOOD period are completely absent from the BAD period AWR (top: 96abn4q2fw1mn, 7uq4jru8y1n3c). These accounted for 443s of elapsed time in the GOOD period. This is strong evidence the batch job or process did NOT complete its primary processing work — it stalled, failed, or was blocked before reaching these execution stages. The lower DB Time in the BAD period reflects less productive work, NOT lower load.
insights.action: 1. Check application/batch job logs for errors or early exits during the BAD period. 2. Verify the job reached its processing stages (scheduler logs, job history). 3. Identify what WAS running in BAD that prevented these SQLs from executing. 4. Compare the execution plans of SQLs that ran in BAD vs GOOD for divergence signs.

insights.title: Incident: Long Running Sql Regression
insights.summary: 4 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL 7tv22qfsdmt4r — 138s → 678s (+109%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
insights.root_cause: 4 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL 7tv22qfsdmt4r — 138s → 678s (+109%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
insights.action: Use Real-Time SQL Monitoring for execution-level visibility. Compare plans: SELECT * FROM TABLE(DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR('<sql_id>')); Gather fresh statistics or use SQL Plan Baseline to lock good plan.

insights.title: Wait Profile Shifted — CPU decreased
insights.summary: The extra DB time is primarily going to CPU (58% → 35%).
insights.root_cause: Wait class 'CPU' changed by -23.0 percentage points.
insights.action: Cross-reference this with the SQL analysis below to find the specific queries driving this shift.

insights.title: Data Volume Increase Detected (2 SQLs)
insights.summary: 2 SQL statements show increased execution counts without per-execution time changes. This indicates data volume growth, not SQL regression. The queries themselves are fine — there's simply more data to process.
insights.root_cause: Data volume increased between the two periods. More rows to process means more executions of the same queries. Check: batch input data size, table row counts, partition growth.
insights.action: Verify data volume change with: SELECT COUNT(*) and partition stats. If volume growth is expected, consider partitioning, parallel DML, or batch scheduling changes. These are NOT SQL tuning candidates — the per-execution time is stable.

insights.summary: 1 existing SQL statements got slower. Worst: 7tv22qfsdmt4r regressed 109%. Check execution plans and statistics freshness.

insights.title: Workload Change: DB Time increased 24%
evidence: Good: DB Time 18.3 min, Elapsed 5.9 min, AAS 3.1
Bad: DB Time 22.8 min, Elapsed 13.8 min, AAS 1.7

insights.action: Review the SQL and wait event analysis below to identify root cause.

verdict.root_cause: CPU is the dominant consumer. This means the database is doing useful work (logical I/O, sorting, PL/SQL). To reduce CPU, optimize the SQL with highest buffer gets per execution.
verdict.confidence_label: Very high — clear bottleneck, direct evidence
verdict_factors: Primary symptom: 'DB CPU' at 57.7% DB time (wait class: CPU)
Object under pressure: LANEEXCEPTION_IDX1 () | Guilty SQL: 96abn4q2fw1mn

analysis_notes: RULE: ADDM recommendations are guides, not gospel. Validate each recommendation against multiple AWR periods before making system changes. Do NOT increase SGA/PGA based on a single AWR report alone — gather at least 3–5 representative periods.
RULE: AWR averages hide spikes. A 60-minute snapshot can mask a 5-minute crisis. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports or use ASH.
RULE: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio is not a primary health signal. A 99% hit ratio can coexist with a burning system. Investigate wait events and per-execution SQL metrics instead.

investigation_trail.finding: AAS = 3.1 vs 32 CPUs — moderate load, within capacity, OS CPU busy: 7%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Database is busy but within CPU capacity. Proceed to bottleneck analysis.

investigation_trail.finding: #1 non-idle wait: 'DB CPU' at 57.7% DB time — this is the symptom.
investigation_trail.conclusion: Primary bottleneck category: CPU. Next: find Top SQL by Elapsed that links to this wait, then check Segment Statistics to confirm which object is under pressure.

investigation_trail.finding: Top SQL 96abn4q2fw1mn at 31% DB time — SQL 96abn4q2fw1mn: 6 execs at 56.9s/exec — low execution count with HIGH per-exec cost. This query IS broken — it needs tuning.
investigation_trail.conclusion: RULE: Always evaluate per-execution cost, not just totals. High total elapsed from many cheap calls = volume problem. High per-exec cost from few calls = broken query. Check execution plans for full-table scans, missing indexes, and bind variable usage.

investigation_trail.finding: Buffer Hit 98.9%, Soft Parse 95.6%, Library Hit 98.3%, Exec to Parse 82.4%, Latch Hit 99.9%
Hard Parses: 12/s (ratio 4.4%), Phys Reads: 6217/s, Executes: 1616/s, Logons: 5.9/s, Commits: 0/s, Redo: 7.7 MB/s
investigation_trail.conclusion: Load profile metrics within normal ranges.

investigation_trail.finding: SQL Execute: 995.9s, PL/SQL: 5.2s, Parse: 20.0s, Hard Parse: 18.0s, Java: 0.0s
Wait class mix: CPU=58%, User I/O=38%, Commit=6%, Network=0%, Other=0%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Wait class distribution looks normal — no single class dominates.

verdict.primary_finding: db file sequential read: 57.1% DB Time (avg 2.8ms)
verdict.root_cause: Single-block I/O: 57.1% DB time, avg 2.8ms, 277,477 waits. Low latency but high volume — the SQL is doing too many index reads.
verdict.confidence_label: Very high — clear bottleneck, direct evidence
verdict_factors: Primary symptom: 'db file sequential read' at 57.1% DB time (wait class: User I/O)
Object under pressure: LANEEXCEPTION_IDX3 () | Guilty SQL: 9xstq8acs539r

analysis_notes: RULE: ADDM recommendations are guides, not gospel. Validate each recommendation against multiple AWR periods before making system changes. Do NOT increase SGA/PGA based on a single AWR report alone — gather at least 3–5 representative periods.
RULE: AWR averages hide spikes. A 60-minute snapshot can mask a 5-minute crisis. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports or use ASH.
RULE: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio is not a primary health signal. A 99% hit ratio can coexist with a burning system. Investigate wait events and per-execution SQL metrics instead.

investigation_trail.finding: AAS = 1.7 vs 32 CPUs — moderate load, within capacity, OS CPU busy: 3%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Database is busy but within CPU capacity. Proceed to bottleneck analysis.

investigation_trail.finding: #1 non-idle wait: 'db file sequential read' at 57.1% DB time — this is the symptom.
investigation_trail.conclusion: Primary bottleneck category: IO. Next: find Top SQL by Elapsed that links to this wait, then check Segment Statistics to confirm which object is under pressure.

investigation_trail.finding: Top SQL 7tv22qfsdmt4r at 50% DB time — SQL 7tv22qfsdmt4r: 184 execs at 3.69s/exec, 8,959 gets/exec.
investigation_trail.conclusion: RULE: Always evaluate per-execution cost, not just totals. High total elapsed from many cheap calls = volume problem. High per-exec cost from few calls = broken query. Check execution plans for full-table scans, missing indexes, and bind variable usage.

investigation_trail.finding: Buffer Hit 99.1%, Soft Parse 96.7%, Library Hit 99.4%, Exec to Parse 89.7%, Latch Hit 99.8%

investigation_trail.conclusion: Load profile metrics within normal ranges.

sql_stats.sql_text: UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ SCPOMGR.SOURCING S1 SET S1.DISC = CASE WHEN TRUNC(S1.EFF) = '01-JAN-1970' THEN :B1 - 1 ELSE S1.EFF END WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MANAGEMENT VMG , SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MAPPING VM WHERE S1.ITEM = VMG.ITEM AND S1.DEST = VMG.DEST AND S1.SOURCE = VMG.VSR AND VM.BACKHAUL_SW = 0 AND VM.VSR = VMG.VSR AND VM.DEST = VMG.DEST AND VM.SUBVENDOR = VMG.SUBVENDOR AND VM.NETCHANGE_SW = 1 AND S1.TRANSMODE LIKE 'BACKHAUL%' )
sql_stats.sql_text_full: UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ SCPOMGR.SOURCING S1 SET S1.DISC = CASE WHEN TRUNC(S1.EFF) = '01-JAN-1970' THEN :B1 - 1 ELSE S1.EFF END WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MANAGEMENT VMG , SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MAPPING VM WHERE S1.ITEM = VMG.ITEM AND S1.DEST = VMG.DEST AND S1.SOURCE = VMG.VSR AND VM.BACKHAUL_SW = 0 AND VM.VSR = VMG.VSR AND VM.DEST = VMG.DEST AND VM.SUBVENDOR = VMG.SUBVENDOR AND VM.NETCHANGE_SW = 1 AND S1.TRANSMODE LIKE 'BACKHAUL%' )

WHERE tc.uom ='95' AND (tc.mincap = 0 AND tc.maxcap = 0) --09/06/23 Added to copy only if both mincap and maxcap is zero for U OM=95 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.transmodecap t, scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.truck_bracket = t.transmode AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 AND vm.u_skuperpalletsw = 1 AND t.uom ='20004' AND t.transmode = tc.transmode AND (t.mincap <> 0 OR t.maxcap <> 0) ); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.TRANSMODECAP MINCAP/MAXCAP UPDATED FOR UOM = 95' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); COMMIT; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); -- Added for AWR--- --APP_SNAPRPT.snapshot_gen;--BY Upgrade: commented --------------------- -------- DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( '-----------------------------------------------------------------------'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'Finishing ' || V_SCRIPT || ' @:' || TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'DD-MM-YYYY HH24:MI:SS')); ---LOG END TIME V_DURATION := ROUND ( ( (DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME - V_DURATION) / 100) / 60, 2); /* CAPTURING LOG DETAILS INTO CDRMGR SCHEMA LOG TABLES*/ INSERT INTO BATCHMGR.BATCH_JOB_AUDIT (JOBID, JOB_NAME, JOB_TYPE, LAST_EXEC, DURATION, SUCCESS_FLAG, RECORDS_PROCESSED, LOG_DESC, SEQUENCE_LOADED) VALUES (C_JOBID, C_JOB_NAME, C_JOB_TYPE, SYSDATE, V_DURATION || ' Min', 'Y', V_PROCESSED_COUNT, C_LOG_MESSAGE, V_SEQUENCE); COMMIT; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( C_JOB_NAME || '-' || 'RUN TIME DETAILS CAPTURED IN BATCHMGR.BATCH_JOB_AUDIT TABLE SUCCESSFULLY'); COMMIT; EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN :RC := '1'; V_ERRDESC := SUBSTR (SQLERRM, 1, 1000); V_ERRCODE := TO_CHAR (SQLCODE); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( C_JOB_NAME || '-' || V_ERRCODE || '-' || V_ERRDESC); V_DURATION := ROUND ( ( (DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME - V_DURATION) / 100) / 60, 2); /* CAPTURING ERROR DETAILS INTO LOG TABLE */ INSERT INTO BATCHMGR.BATCH_JOB_AUDIT (JOBID, JOB_NAME, JOB_TYPE, LAST_EXEC, DURATION, SUCCESS_FLAG, RECORDS_PROCESSED, LOG_DESC, SEQUENCE_LOADED) VALUES ( C_JOBID, C_JOB_NAME, C_JOB_TYPE, SYSDA TE, V_DURATION || ' Min', 'N', V_PROCESSED_COUNT, C_JOB_NAME || ' ' || 'FAILED' || '-' || 'ORACLE ERROR NUMBER - ' || V_ERRCODE || '-' || V_ERRDESC, V_SEQUENCE); COMMIT; END;

09rj8cj1nxg06.fullText: UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ SCPOMGR.SOURCING S1 SET S1.DISC = CASE WHEN TRUNC(S1.EFF) = '01-JAN-1970' THEN :B1 - 1 ELSE S1.EFF END WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MANAGEMENT VMG , SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MAPPING VM WHERE S1.ITEM = VMG.ITEM AND S1.DEST = VMG.DEST AND S1.SOURCE = VMG.VSR AND VM.BACKHAUL_SW = 0 AND VM.VSR = VMG.VSR AND VM.DEST = VMG.DEST AND VM.SUBVENDOR = VMG.SUBVENDOR AND VM.NETCHANGE_SW = 1 AND S1.TRANSMODE LIKE 'BACKHAUL%' )

5gr3a3v64hkvv.fullText: DECLARE V_SCRIPT VARCHAR2 (50); V_DURATION NUMBER; /* TO STORE TIME STAMP */ C_JOBID CONSTANT VARCHAR2 (50) := 'JBN203A'; /* ASSING THE JOBID TO A VARIABLE */ V_ERRCODE VARCHAR2 (50); /* TO CAPTURE ORACLE ERROR CODE */ V_ERRDESC VARCHAR2 (1000); /* TO CAPTURE ORACLE ERROR DESCRIPTION */ V_SEQUENCE NUMBER; V_PROC_STATUS NUMBER; C_JOB_NAME CONSTANT VARCHAR2 (50) := 'Update ordering params based on NETCHANGESW'; /* TO STORE INTERFACE NAME */ C_JOB_TYPE CONSTANT VARCHAR2 (100) := 'UPDATE'; /* TO STORE TYPE OF INTERFACE */ V_PROCESSED_COUNT NUMBER := 0; /* TOTAL NUMBER OF RECORDS PROCESSED BY THE JOB */ C_LOG_MESSAGE VARCHAR2 (500) := 'SUCCESSFUL'; D_RUNDATE DATE; -- To store the current date ------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- BEGIN V_SCRIPT := 'JBN203A_UPDATE_ORDERPARAMS_NETCHANGE.SQL'; /* CAPTURING START TIME */ V_DURATION := DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( '-----------------------------------------------------------------------'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'Starting ' || V_SCRIPT || ' @:' || TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'DD-MM-YYYY HH24:MI:SS')); :RC := 0; --INITIALIZE THE RETURN CODE TO 0 EXECUTE IMMEDIATE 'ALTER SESSION ENABLE PARALLEL DML'; SELECT TO_DATE (param_value, 'RRRRMMDD') INTO d_rundate FROM SCPOMGR.udt_batch_param WHERE param_name = 'PROCESSDATE' AND param_group = 'SCPO'; UPDATE scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm SET netchange_sw = 1 WHERE (process_sw = 1 OR U_RIO_SW = 1 ); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'Number of Records where process_sw set to 1 : ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); /* UPDATE scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm SET netchange_sw = 0 WHERE process_sw = 0; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'Number of Records where netchange_sw set to 0 : ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records');*/ -- Set the Process status UPDATE scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping SET process_status = 'In Progress' WHERE netchange_sw = 1 ; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'Number of Records Picked for Processing: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); --Reset the backhaul_sw to 0 UPDATE scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm SET vm.backhaul_sw = 0 WHERE vm.backhaul_cnt = 0 AND vm.backhaul_sw = 1 ; /* --Reset the backhaul_sw to 1 UPDATE scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg SET backhaul_sw = 0 WHERE backhaul_bracket = ' ' AND backhaul_sw = 1 ; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MAPPING.BACKHAUL_SW UPDATED TO 0: ' || SQ L%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); */ --Set the backhaul_SW to 1 UPDATE scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vmg SET backhaul_sw = 1 WHERE backhaul_cnt > 0 AND netchange_sw = 1; /* UPDATE scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg SET backhaul_cnt = 1 WHERE backhaul_bracket <> ' ' AND backhaul_cnt = 0; */ DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'BACKHAUL SW UPDATED to 0 WHERE IT WAS 1 AND BACKHAUL CNT IS 0 : ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; ----Set the backhaul count to 0 when backhaul is scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg, scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vmg.item = sc.item AND vmg.vsr = sc.source AND vmg.dest = sc.dest AND vm.truck_bracket = sc.transmode AND vm.backhaul_sw = 1 AND vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest AND vm.subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.sourcinguomconvfactor scf WHERE scf.item = sc.item AND scf.source = sc.source AND scf.dest = sc.dest AND scf.transmode = CASE WHEN vmg.subvendor = ' ' then 'BACKHAUL_' ||vmg.backhaul_bracket WHEN vmg.subvendor <> ' ' THEN 'BACKHAUL_'||vmg.subvendor||'_'||vmg.backhaul_bracket end AND scf.sourcecategory = sc.sourcecategory AND scf.targetcategory = sc.targetcategory ); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SOURCINGUOM CREATED FOR BACKHAUL: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); */ -- Update the orderoptsw to 1 for backhaul and netchange records UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ scpomgr.sourcing s SET u_orderopt_sw = 1 WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vmg WHERE s.source = vmg.vsr AND s.dest = vmg.dest AND (vmg.netchange_sw = 1) ) AND trunc(s.disc) = '01-JAN-1970'; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SOURCING.U_ORDEROPT_SW UPDATED TO 1: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; --Update the sourcing .u_backhaul_vnd to 1 UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ scpomgr.sourcing s SET u_backhaul_vnd = 1 WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg , scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE s.item = vmg.item AND s.source = vmg.vsr AND s.dest = vmg.dest AND s.u_subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.backhaul_sw = 1 AND vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest AND vm.subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 ) ; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SOURCING.U_BACKHAUL_VND UPDATED TO 1: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; --Update the sourcing.u_backhaul_vnd to 0 UPDATE scpomgr.sourcing s SET u_backhaul_vnd = 0 WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg , scpomgr .udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE s.item = vmg.item AND s.source = vmg.vsr AND s.dest = vmg.dest AND s.u_subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.backhaul_sw = 0 AND vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest AND vm.subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.netchange_sw = 1); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SOURCING.U_BACKHAUL_VND UPDATED TO 0: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; UPDATE scpomgr.network n SET ( autoapprovalsw, autoapprovalprofile ) = (SELECT u_auto_approval_sw, u_auto_approval_profile FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.vsr = n.source AND vm.dest = n.dest AND vm.subvendor = ' ' AND vm.supervendor = ' ' AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 ) WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.vsr = n.source AND vm.dest = n.dest AND vm.subvendor = ' ' AND vm.supervendor = ' ' AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 ) LOG ERRORS INTO scpomgr.ERR$_NETWORK('Update Query NETWORK') REJECT LIMIT 500; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.NETWORK.AUTOPPROVALSW AND AUTOAPPROVALPROFILE UPDATED: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; UPDATE scpomgr.network n SET u_rio_sw = 1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE n.source = vm.vsr AND n.dest = vm.dest AND vm.u_rio_Sw = 1 -- #1.1 AND n.transmode = vm.truck_bracket /*Removed the condition for RIO to work) ); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.NETWORK.U_RIO_SW = 1 UPDATED: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; UPDATE /*+ parallel(8) */ scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm SET skucount = 0 WHERE dest_type ='DC'; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SKUCOUNT RESET FOR DC: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; MERGE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ INTO scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm USING ( SELECT COUNT(*) cnt, vsr, dest FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg , scpomgr.sku sk WHERE vmg.vsr = sk.u_vsr AND vmg.dest = sk.loc AND sk.u_lane_type in ('DC-VEN') AND sk.u_discflag = 0 AND sk.item = vmg.item AND vmg.subvendor = ' ' AND vmg.dest_type = 'DC' GROUP BY vsr, dest ) vmg ON ( vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest ) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET vm.skucount = vmg.cnt; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SKUCOUNT UPDATED FOR MOTHER VENDOR: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS ')); COMMIT; MERGE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ INTO scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm USING ( SELECT COUNT(*) cnt, vsr, dest, subvendor FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg , scpomgr.sku sk where subvendor <> ' ' AND vmg.vsr = sk.u_vsr AND vmg.dest = sk.loc AND sk.u_lane_type in ('DC-VEN') AND sk.u_discflag = 0 AND sk.item = vmg.item AND vmg.dest_type ='DC' GROUP BY vsr, dest, subvendor ) vmg ON ( vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest AND vm.subvendor= vmg.subvendor ) --- removed netchange condition 11-jan-23 WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET vm.skucount = vmg.cnt; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SKUCOUNT UPDATED FOR SUBVENDOR: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; MERGE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ INTO scpomgr. udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm USING ( SELECT vsr, dest, SUM(skucount) cnt FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.subvendor <> ' ' AND vm.dest_type ='DC' GROUP BY vm.vsr, vm.dest ) x ON ( x.vsr = vm.vsr AND x.dest = vm.dest AND vm.subvendor = ' ' ) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET vm.skucount = CASE WHEN x.cnt >= vm.skucount THEN 0 ELSE vm.skucount END; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SKUCOUNT UPDATED FOR MOTHERVENDOR WITHOUT ANY ITEM: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; MERGE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ INTO SCPOMGR.skuplanningparam skp USING (SELECT distinct vmg.item, vmg.dest, (vm.wh_leadtime + 1 )* 1440 dur FROM SCPOMGR.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm , scpomgr.udt_f f_vendor_management vmg WHERE vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest and vm.subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 ) x ON (skp.item = x.item and skp.loc = x.dest ) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET skp.drpcovdur = x.dur; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM DRPCOVDUR UPDATED: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; ----1.3 UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ scpomgr.transmodecap tc SET tc.checkcapsw = 1 WHERE tc.UOM = 95 AND tc.transmode in (SELECT truck_bracket FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.u_skuperpalletsw = 1 AND vm.netchange_sw = 1) AND tc.checkcapsw = 0 ; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.TRANSMODECAP CHECKCAPSW UPDATED TO 1 FOR UOM 95 ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ scpomgr.transmodecap tc SET tc.checkcaps w = 0 WHERE tc.UOM <> 95 AND tc.transmode in (SELECT truck_bracket FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.u_skuperpalletsw = 1 AND vm.netchange_sw = 1) AND tc.checkcapsw = 1 ; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.TRANSMODECAP CHECKCAPSW UPDATED TO 0 FOR UOM <> 95' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); COMMIT; UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ scpomgr.transmodecap tc SET ( tc.mincap, tc.maxcap ) = (SELECT distinct mincap, maxcap FROM scpomgr.transmodecap t, scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.truck_bracket = t.transmode AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 AND vm.u_skuperpalletsw = 1 AND t.uom ='20004' AND t.transmode = tc.transmode AND (t.mincap <> 0 OR t.maxcap <> 0) --09/06/23 Added to copy only if either mincap or maxcap is non-zero for UOM = 2004 ) WHERE tc.uom ='95' AND (tc.mincap = 0 AND tc.maxcap = 0) --09/06/23 Added to copy only if both mincap and maxcap is zero for U OM=95 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.transmodecap t, scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.truck_bracket = t.transmode AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 AND vm.u_skuperpalletsw = 1 AND t.uom ='20004' AND t.transmode = tc.transmode AND (t.mincap <> 0 OR t.maxcap <> 0) ); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.TRANSMODECAP MINCAP/MAXCAP UPDATED FOR UOM = 95' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); COMMIT; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); -- Added for AWR--- --APP_SNAPRPT.snapshot_gen;--BY Upgrade: commented --------------------- -------- DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( '-----------------------------------------------------------------------'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'Finishing ' || V_SCRIPT || ' @:' || TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'DD-MM-YYYY HH24:MI:SS')); ---LOG END TIME V_DURATION := ROUND ( ( (DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME - V_DURATION) / 100) / 60, 2); /* CAPTURING LOG DETAILS INTO CDRMGR SCHEMA LOG TABLES*/ INSERT INTO BATCHMGR.BATCH_JOB_AUDIT (JOBID, JOB_NAME, JOB_TYPE, LAST_EXEC, DURATION, SUCCESS_FLAG, RECORDS_PROCESSED, LOG_DESC, SEQUENCE_LOADED) VALUES (C_JOBID, C_JOB_NAME, C_JOB_TYPE, SYSDATE, V_DURATION || ' Min', 'Y', V_PROCESSED_COUNT, C_LOG_MESSAGE, V_SEQUENCE); COMMIT; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( C_JOB_NAME || '-' || 'RUN TIME DETAILS CAPTURED IN BATCHMGR.BATCH_JOB_AUDIT TABLE SUCCESSFULLY'); COMMIT; EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN :RC := '1'; V_ERRDESC := SUBSTR (SQLERRM, 1, 1000); V_ERRCODE := TO_CHAR (SQLCODE); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( C_JOB_NAME || '-' || V_ERRCODE || '-' || V_ERRDESC); V_DURATION := ROUND ( ( (DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME - V_DURATION) / 100) / 60, 2); /* CAPTURING ERROR DETAILS INTO LOG TABLE */ INSERT INTO BATCHMGR.BATCH_JOB_AUDIT (JOBID, JOB_NAME, JOB_TYPE, LAST_EXEC, DURATION, SUCCESS_FLAG, RECORDS_PROCESSED, LOG_DESC, SEQUENCE_LOADED) VALUES ( C_JOBID, C_JOB_NAME, C_JOB_TYPE, SYSDA TE, V_DURATION || ' Min', 'N', V_PROCESSED_COUNT, C_JOB_NAME || ' ' || 'FAILED' || '-' || 'ORACLE ERROR NUMBER - ' || V_ERRCODE || '-' || V_ERRDESC, V_SEQUENCE); COMMIT; END;

, SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT.DESCR as DMDUNIT_DESCR , SCPOMGR.LOC.DESCR as LOC_DESCR , SCPOMGR.LOC.LOC_TYPE as LOC_LOC_TYPE , SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT.U_DEPT as DMDUNIT_U_DEPT , SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT.U_SUBDEPT as FCSTDRAFTNARROW_QTY , SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW.U_ROLLOUTSW as DFUVIEW_U_ROLLOUTSW , SCPOMGR.LOC.U_COUNTRYDESCR as LOC_U_COUNTRYDESCR , SCPOMGR.LOC.U_DIVISIONDESCR as LOC_U_DIVISIONDESCR , SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER as DFUVIEW_U_PLANNER , SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW.U_E3CLASS as DFUVIEW_U_E3CLASS , SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW.U_E3BUYERCLASS as DFUVIEW_U_E3BUYERCLASS , SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW.U_PLANNING_SYSTEM as DFUVIEW_U_PLANNING_SYSTEM , SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT.U_ARTICLE as DMDU NIT_U_ARTICLE , SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW.TYPE as FCSTDRAFTNARROW_TYPE , SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW.FCSTID as FCSTDRAFTNARROW_FCSTID FROM SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT, SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW, SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW, SCPOMGR.LOC, SCPOMGR.DFUEXCEPTION RELX_DFUEXCEPTION, SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW RELX_DFUVIEW, SCPOMGR.LEWANDOWSKISTATS RELX_LEWANDOWSKISTATS WHERE ( SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW.U_ROLLOUTSW = 1 ) AND SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW.DMDUNIT=SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT.DMDUNIT AND SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW.DMDUNIT=SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW.DMDUNIT AND SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW.DMDGROUP=SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW.DMDGROUP AND SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW.LOC=SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW.LOC AND SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW.LOC=SCPOMGR.LOC.LOC and (SCPOMGR.LOC.LOC_TYPE = 6 AND RELX_DFUEXCEPTION.DESCR = 'Extreme forecast value(s) encountered.' AND (RELX_DFUVIEW.U_E3BUYERCLASS = 'R' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_E3BUYERCLASS = 'W') AND RELX_DFUVIEW.U_DISCFLAG = 0 AND (RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'CKRUGER' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'AAHMED' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'BDOYLE' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'TPRAMMONEY' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'LCLANGA' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'NTEMERE' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'IROBINSON' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'IDODGEN' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'JDCARDOSO' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'DCORDY' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'AJAJULA' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'PCAROLUS' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'PTSHILONGO' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'XMSILA' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'NVANAARD' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'NCHENGAN' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'TKGATLE' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'ANGXANGA' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'GLBATCHELOR' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'SDONDOLO' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'BMBANGA' OR RELX_DFUVIEW.U_PLANNER = 'SMOODLEY') AND RELX_LEWANDOWSKISTATS.MEAN >= 1.0 AND (SCPOMGR.LOC.U_COUNTRYDESCR = 'Angola' OR SCPOMGR.LOC.U_COUNTRYDESCR = 'South Africa') AND SCPOMGR.LOC.U_FRANCHISE_SW = 0) and SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW.DMDUNIT=RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDUNIT AND SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW.DMD GROUP=RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDGROUP AND SCPOMGR.FCSTDRAFTNARROW.LOC=RELX_DFUVIEW.LOC and RELX_DFUVIEW.LOC=SCPOMGR.LOC.LOC and RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDUNIT=RELX_DFUEXCEPTION.DMDUNIT AND RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDGROUP=RELX_DFUEXCEPTION.DMDGROUP AND RELX_DFUVIEW.LOC=RELX_DFUEXCEPTION.LOC and RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDUNIT=RELX_LEWANDOWSKISTATS.DMDUNIT AND RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDGROUP=RELX_LEWANDOWSKISTATS.DMDGROUP AND RELX_DFUVIEW.LOC=RELX_LEWANDOWSKISTATS.LOC and RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDUNIT=SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT.DMDUNIT) GROUP BY FCSTDRAFTNARROW_DMDGROUP , FCSTDRAFTNARROW_DMDUNIT , FCSTDRAFTNARROW_LOC , FCSTDRAFTNARROW_STARTDATE , FCSTDRAFTNARROW_MODEL , DFUVIEW_U_DC , DMDUNIT_U_UPC , DMDUNIT_DESCR , LOC_DESCR , LOC_LOC_TYPE , DMDUNIT_U_DEPT , DMDUNIT_U_SUBDEPT , DMDUNIT_U_CAT , DMDUNIT_U_CATDESCR , DMDUNIT_U_SUBCAT , DFUVIEW_U_ROLLOUTSW , LOC_U_COUNTRYDESCR , LOC_U_DIVISIONDESCR , DFUVIEW_U_PLANNER , DFUVIEW_U_E3CLASS , DFUVIEW_U_E3BUYERCLASS , DFUVIEW_U_PLANNING_SYSTEM , DMDUNIT_U_ARTICLE ORDER BY FCSTDRAFT NARROW_STARTDATE ASC

7281nbf37drtq.fullText: INSERT /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ INTO SCPOMGR.TRANSMODECAP ( TRANSMODE, MINCAP, MAXCAP, UOM, CHECKCAPSW ) SELECT TRANSMODE, 0 MINCAP, 0 MAXCAP, UOM, 0 CHECKCAPSW FROM SCPOMGR.UOM U, SCPOMGR.TRANSMODE T WHERE UOM IN ( '20001', '20002', '20003', '20004', '20005', '20006', '20015', '20016', '20050', '95', '43', '53' ) AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM SCPOMGR.TRANSMODECAP TC WHERE TC.TRANSMODE = T.TRANSMODE AND TC.UOM = U.UOM )

9hxy0f8atjby8.fullText: UPDATE SCPOMGR.VEHICLELOADLINE VLL SET ( VLL.SCHEDSHIPDATE, VLL.SCHEDARRIVDATE ) = ( SELECT SHIPDATE, ARRIVDATE FROM SCPOMGR.VEHICLELOAD VL WHERE VL.EXPORTED = 0 AND VL.APPROVALSTATUS = 1 AND VL.LOADID = VLL.LOADID ) WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM SCPOMGR.VEHICLELOAD VL WHERE VL.EXPORTED = 0 AND VL.APPROVALSTATUS = 1 AND VL.LOADID = VLL.LOADID AND VLL.SOURCE IN ( SELECT LOC FROM SCPOMGR.LOC WHERE U_LOC_TYPE = 'VENDOR' ) AND VLL.DEST IN ( SELECT LOC FROM SCPOMGR.LOC WHERE U_LOC_TYPE IN ( 'DC')))

bqmzynabugvgg.fullText: UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ SCPOMGR.SOURCING S SET S.TRANSMODE = NVL((SELECT DISTINCT VM.TRUCK_BRACKET FROM SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MANAGEMENT VMG , SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MAPPING VM WHERE VM.NETCHANGE_SW = 1 AND S.ITEM = VMG.ITEM AND S.SOURCE = VMG.VSR AND S.DEST = VMG.DEST AND S.TRANSMODE <> VM.TRUCK_BRACKET AND VMG.VSR = VM.VSR AND VMG.DEST= VM.DEST AND S.TRANSMODE NOT LIKE 'BACKHAUL%' AND S.TRANSMODE <> 'TRUCK_RIO' AND VMG.SUBVENDOR = VM.SUBVENDOR ), S.TRANSMODE) WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MANAGEMENT VMG , SCPOMGR.UDT_FF_VENDOR_MAPPING VM WHERE VM.NETCHANGE_SW = 1 AND S.ITEM = VMG.ITEM AND S.SOURCE = VMG.VSR AND S.DEST = VMG.DEST AND S.TRANSMODE <> VM.TRUCK_BRACKET AND VMG.VSR = VM.VSR AND VMG.DEST= VM.DEST AND VMG.SUBVENDOR = VM.SUBVENDOR )

cr9wu61faktg3.fullText: -- SRE statement details select a.status, a.module, a.sql_id, event, pdml_status PDML, pddl_status PDDL, pq_status PQ, pdml_enabled isPDML, last_call_et RRT, count(1) sessions, sql_text from gv$session a, (select distinct sql_text, sql_id, module from gv$sql) c where a.sql_id=c.sql_id(+) and a.client_identifier like '%835403' -- SRE --and status='ACTIVE' group by a.status, a.module, a.sql_id, event, pdml_status, pddl_status, pq_status, last_call_et, sql_text, pdml_enabled order by sql_id, event

vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest AND vm.subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 AND s1.transmode like 'BACKHAUL%' --- Added on 07/15 ); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SOURCING BACKHAUL TO DISC WHEN BACHAULSW is 0(SUBVENDOR DELETION): ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; /*INSERT INTO scpomgr.networkcap ( uom, mincap, source, dest, transmode ) SELECT DISTINCT nc.uom, nc.mincap, nc.source, nc.dest, CASE WHEN subvendor = ' ' THEN 'BACKHAUL_' || vmg.backhaul_bracket WHEN subvendor <> ' ' THEN 'BACKHAUL' || '_' ||vmg.subvendor||'_'|| vmg.backhaul_bracket end transmode FROM scpomgr.networkcap nc, scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vmg WHERE nc.source = vmg.vsr AND nc.dest = vmg.dest AND nc.transmode = vmg.truck_bracket AND vmg.backhaul_sw = 1 AND vmg.netchange_sw = 1 -- AND vmg.subvendor = ' ' AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.networkcap n WHERE n.uom = nc.uom AND n.source = nc.source AND n.dest = nc.dest AND n.transmode = CASE WHEN subvendor = ' ' THEN 'BACKHAUL_' || vmg.backhaul_bracket WHEN subvendor <> ' ' THEN 'BACKHAUL' || '_' ||vmg.subvendor||'_'|| vmg.backhaul_bracket end ); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.NETWORKCAP POPULATED FOR BACKHAUL: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); */ /* INSERT INTO scpomgr.sourcinguomconvfactor ( ratio, sourceuom, targetuom, source, dest, transmode, sourcecategory, targetcategory, item ) SELECT DISTINCT sc.ratio, sc.sourceuom, sc.targetuom, sc.source, sc.dest, CASE WHEN vmg.subvendor = ' ' then 'BACKHAUL_' ||vmg.backhaul_bracket WHEN vmg.subvendor <> ' ' THEN 'BACKHAUL_'||vmg.subvendor||'_'||vmg.backhaul_bracket end transmode, sc.sourcecategory, sc.targetcategory, sc.item FROM scpomgr.sourcinguomconvfactor sc, scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg, scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vmg.item = sc.item AND vmg.vsr = sc.source AND vmg.dest = sc.dest AND vm.truck_bracket = sc.transmode AND vm.backhaul_sw = 1 AND vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest AND vm.subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.sourcinguomconvfactor scf WHERE scf.item = sc.item AND scf.source = sc.source AND scf.dest = sc.dest AND scf.transmode = CASE WHEN vmg.subvendor = ' ' then 'BACKHAUL_' ||vmg.backhaul_bracket WHEN vmg.subvendor <> ' ' THEN 'BACKHAUL_'||vmg.subvendor||'_'||vmg.backhaul_bracket end AND scf.sourcecategory = sc.sourcecategory AND scf.targetcategory = sc.targetcategory ); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SOURCINGUOM CREATED FOR BACKHAUL: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); */ -- Update the orderoptsw to 1 for backhaul and netchange records UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ scpomgr.sourcing s SET u_orderopt_sw = 1 WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vmg WHERE s.source = vmg.vsr AND s.dest = vmg.dest AND (vmg.netchange_sw = 1) ) AND trunc(s.disc) = '01-JAN-1970'; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SOURCING.U_ORDEROPT_SW UPDATED TO 1: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; --Update the sourcing .u_backhaul_vnd to 1 UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ scpomgr.sourcing s SET u_backhaul_vnd = 1 WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg , scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE s.item = vmg.item AND s.source = vmg.vsr AND s.dest = vmg.dest AND s.u_subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.backhaul_sw = 1 AND vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest AND vm.subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 ) ; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SOURCING.U_BACKHAUL_VND UPDATED TO 1: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; --Update the sourcing.u_backhaul_vnd to 0 UPDATE scpomgr.sourcing s SET u_backhaul_vnd = 0 WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg , scpomgr .udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE s.item = vmg.item AND s.source = vmg.vsr AND s.dest = vmg.dest AND s.u_subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.backhaul_sw = 0 AND vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest AND vm.subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.netchange_sw = 1); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SOURCING.U_BACKHAUL_VND UPDATED TO 0: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; UPDATE scpomgr.network n SET ( autoapprovalsw, autoapprovalprofile ) = (SELECT u_auto_approval_sw, u_auto_approval_profile FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.vsr = n.source AND vm.dest = n.dest AND vm.subvendor = ' ' AND vm.supervendor = ' ' AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 ) WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.vsr = n.source AND vm.dest = n.dest AND vm.subvendor = ' ' AND vm.supervendor = ' ' AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 ) LOG ERRORS INTO scpomgr.ERR$_NETWORK('Update Query NETWORK') REJECT LIMIT 500; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.NETWORK.AUTOPPROVALSW AND AUTOAPPROVALPROFILE UPDATED: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; UPDATE scpomgr.network n SET u_rio_sw = 1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE n.source = vm.vsr AND n.dest = vm.dest AND vm.u_rio_Sw = 1 -- #1.1 AND n.transmode = vm.truck_bracket /*Removed the condition for RIO to work) ); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.NETWORK.U_RIO_SW = 1 UPDATED: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; UPDATE /*+ parallel(8) */ scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm SET skucount = 0 WHERE dest_type ='DC'; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SKUCOUNT RESET FOR DC: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; MERGE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ INTO scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm USING ( SELECT COUNT(*) cnt, vsr, dest FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg , scpomgr.sku sk WHERE vmg.vsr = sk.u_vsr AND vmg.dest = sk.loc AND sk.u_lane_type in ('DC-VEN') AND sk.u_discflag = 0 AND sk.item = vmg.item AND vmg.subvendor = ' ' AND vmg.dest_type = 'DC' GROUP BY vsr, dest ) vmg ON ( vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest ) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET vm.skucount = vmg.cnt; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SKUCOUNT UPDATED FOR MOTHER VENDOR: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS ')); COMMIT; MERGE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ INTO scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm USING ( SELECT COUNT(*) cnt, vsr, dest, subvendor FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_management vmg , scpomgr.sku sk where subvendor <> ' ' AND vmg.vsr = sk.u_vsr AND vmg.dest = sk.loc AND sk.u_lane_type in ('DC-VEN') AND sk.u_discflag = 0 AND sk.item = vmg.item AND vmg.dest_type ='DC' GROUP BY vsr, dest, subvendor ) vmg ON ( vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest AND vm.subvendor= vmg.subvendor ) --- removed netchange condition 11-jan-23 WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET vm.skucount = vmg.cnt; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SKUCOUNT UPDATED FOR SUBVENDOR: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; MERGE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ INTO scpomgr. udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm USING ( SELECT vsr, dest, SUM(skucount) cnt FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.subvendor <> ' ' AND vm.dest_type ='DC' GROUP BY vm.vsr, vm.dest ) x ON ( x.vsr = vm.vsr AND x.dest = vm.dest AND vm.subvendor = ' ' ) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET vm.skucount = CASE WHEN x.cnt >= vm.skucount THEN 0 ELSE vm.skucount END; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SKUCOUNT UPDATED FOR MOTHERVENDOR WITHOUT ANY ITEM: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; MERGE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ INTO SCPOMGR.skuplanningparam skp USING (SELECT distinct vmg.item, vmg.dest, (vm.wh_leadtime + 1 )* 1440 dur FROM SCPOMGR.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm , scpomgr.udt_f f_vendor_management vmg WHERE vm.vsr = vmg.vsr AND vm.dest = vmg.dest and vm.subvendor = vmg.subvendor AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 ) x ON (skp.item = x.item and skp.loc = x.dest ) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET skp.drpcovdur = x.dur; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM DRPCOVDUR UPDATED: ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; ----1.3 UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ scpomgr.transmodecap tc SET tc.checkcapsw = 1 WHERE tc.UOM = 95 AND tc.transmode in (SELECT truck_bracket FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.u_skuperpalletsw = 1 AND vm.netchange_sw = 1) AND tc.checkcapsw = 0 ; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.TRANSMODECAP CHECKCAPSW UPDATED TO 1 FOR UOM 95 ' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); COMMIT; UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ scpomgr.transmodecap tc SET tc.checkcaps w = 0 WHERE tc.UOM <> 95 AND tc.transmode in (SELECT truck_bracket FROM scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.u_skuperpalletsw = 1 AND vm.netchange_sw = 1) AND tc.checkcapsw = 1 ; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.TRANSMODECAP CHECKCAPSW UPDATED TO 0 FOR UOM <> 95' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); COMMIT; UPDATE /*+ PARALLEL(8) */ scpomgr.transmodecap tc SET ( tc.mincap, tc.maxcap ) = (SELECT distinct mincap, maxcap FROM scpomgr.transmodecap t, scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.truck_bracket = t.transmode AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 AND vm.u_skuperpalletsw = 1 AND t.uom ='20004' AND t.transmode = tc.transmode AND (t.mincap <> 0 OR t.maxcap <> 0) --09/06/23 Added to copy only if either mincap or maxcap is non-zero for UOM = 2004 ) WHERE tc.uom ='95' AND (tc.mincap = 0 AND tc.maxcap = 0) --09/06/23 Added to copy only if both mincap and maxcap is zero for U OM=95 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM scpomgr.transmodecap t, scpomgr.udt_ff_vendor_mapping vm WHERE vm.truck_bracket = t.transmode AND vm.netchange_sw = 1 AND vm.u_skuperpalletsw = 1 AND t.uom ='20004' AND t.transmode = tc.transmode AND (t.mincap <> 0 OR t.maxcap <> 0) ); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'SCPOMGR.TRANSMODECAP MINCAP/MAXCAP UPDATED FOR UOM = 95' || SQL%ROWCOUNT ||' Records'); COMMIT; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')); -- Added for AWR--- --APP_SNAPRPT.snapshot_gen;--BY Upgrade: commented --------------------- -------- DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( '-----------------------------------------------------------------------'); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( 'Finishing ' || V_SCRIPT || ' @:' || TO_CHAR (SYSDATE, 'DD-MM-YYYY HH24:MI:SS')); ---LOG END TIME V_DURATION := ROUND ( ( (DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME - V_DURATION) / 100) / 60, 2); /* CAPTURING LOG DETAILS INTO CDRMGR SCHEMA LOG TABLES*/ INSERT INTO BATCHMGR.BATCH_JOB_AUDIT (JOBID, JOB_NAME, JOB_TYPE, LAST_EXEC, DURATION, SUCCESS_FLAG, RECORDS_PROCESSED, LOG_DESC, SEQUENCE_LOADED) VALUES (C_JOBID, C_JOB_NAME, C_JOB_TYPE, SYSDATE, V_DURATION || ' Min', 'Y', V_PROCESSED_COUNT, C_LOG_MESSAGE, V_SEQUENCE); COMMIT; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( C_JOB_NAME || '-' || 'RUN TIME DETAILS CAPTURED IN BATCHMGR.BATCH_JOB_AUDIT TABLE SUCCESSFULLY'); COMMIT; EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN :RC := '1'; V_ERRDESC := SUBSTR (SQLERRM, 1, 1000); V_ERRCODE := TO_CHAR (SQLCODE); DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ( C_JOB_NAME || '-' || V_ERRCODE || '-' || V_ERRDESC); V_DURATION := ROUND ( ( (DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME - V_DURATION) / 100) / 60, 2); /* CAPTURING ERROR DETAILS INTO LOG TABLE */ INSERT INTO BATCHMGR.BATCH_JOB_AUDIT (JOBID, JOB_NAME, JOB_TYPE, LAST_EXEC, DURATION, SUCCESS_FLAG, RECORDS_PROCESSED, LOG_DESC, SEQUENCE_LOADED) VALUES ( C_JOBID, C_JOB_NAME, C_JOB_TYPE, SYSDA TE, V_DURATION || ' Min', 'N', V_PROCESSED_COUNT, C_JOB_NAME || ' ' || 'FAILED' || '-' || 'ORACLE ERROR NUMBER - ' || V_ERRCODE || '-' || V_ERRDESC, V_SEQUENCE); COMMIT; END;

ROUND(100 * (GREATEST(MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES, MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES) - MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES) / MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 2))) END) ) END) FROM MONITOR_AGG MA) ELSE NULL END) FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN V1.XPLAN_XML IS NULL OR V1.XPLAN_XML.EXISTSNODE('/error') > 0 THEN NULL ELSE V1.XPLAN_XML END XPLAN_XML FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN :B36 = 1 THEN DBMS_XPLAN.BUILD_PLAN_XML( TABLE_NAME=>'gv$all_sql_plan', PLAN_TAG=>'plan', FILTER_PREDS=>:B35 , FORMAT=>'-PROJECTION +ALIAS +ADAPTIVE') ELSE NULL END XP LAN_XML FROM DUAL) V1) CONST_VIEW

SCPOMGR.NETWORK RELX_NETWORK, SCPOMGR.SOURCING RELX_SOURCING, SCPOMGR.ORDERGROUPMEMBER RELX_ORDERGROUPMEMBER, SCPOMGR.ITEM RELX_ITEM where ( UPPER(RELX_SOURCING.SOURCE) LIKE '631780-07' AND UPPER(RELX_SOURCING.DEST) LIKE '3994' AND RELX_SOURCING.U_BACKHAUL_VND = 0) and SCPOMGR.ORDERHEADER.ORDERID=RELX_ORDERSKU.ORDERID AND RELX_ORDERSKU.ITEM=RELX_SOURCING.ITEM AND RELX_ORDERSKU.SOURCE=RELX_SOURCING.SOURCE AND RELX_ORDERSKU.DEST=RELX_SOURCING.DEST AND RELX_ORDERSKU.TRANSMODE=RELX_SOURCING.TRANSMODE and RELX_SOURCING.DEST=RELX_NETWORK.DEST AND RELX_SOURCING.SOURCE=RELX_NETWORK.SOURCE AND RELX_SOURCING.TRANSMODE=RELX_NETWORK.TRANSMODE and RELX_SOURCING.DEST=RELX_LOC.LOC and RELX_SOURCING.ITEM=RELX_ITEM.ITEM and RELX_SOURCING.ORDERGROUP=RELX_ORDERGROUPMEMBER.ORDERGROUP AND RELX_SOURCING.ORDERGROUPMEMBER=RELX_ORDERGROUPMEMBER.ORDERGROUPMEMBER ORDER BY SCPOMGR.ORDERHEADER.DEST, SCPO MGR.ORDERHEADER.ORDERPLACEDATE ) where rownum < 100002), SCPOMGR.ORDERGROUPMEMBER, SCPOMGR.LOC, SCPOMGR.NETWORK, SCPOMGR.ORDERHEADER where orderheader.rowid = RID1 and SCPOMGR.ORDERHEADER.ORDERGROUP=SCPOMGR.ORDERGROUPMEMBER.ORDERGROUP (+) AND SCPOMGR.ORDERHEADER.ORDERGROUPMEMBER=SCPOMGR.ORDERGROUPMEMBER.ORDERGROUPMEMBER (+) and SCPOMGR.ORDERHEADER.DEST=SCPOMGR.LOC.LOC (+) and SCPOMGR.ORDERHEADER.SOURCE=SCPOMGR.NETWORK.SOURCE (+) AND SCPOMGR.ORDERHEADER.DEST=SCPOMGR.NETWORK.DEST (+) AND SCPOMGR.ORDERHEADER.TRANSMODE=SCPOMGR.NETWORK.TRANSMODE (+) ORDER BY 8, 13, 1 DESC

7x6fdjsw5xm9z.fullText: SELECT /* InvOpt.ServiceProfileSelectLiveHint */ IOSERVICEPROFILE, MINCSL, MAXCSL, MINCWT, MAXCWT, MINRQSNCWT, MAXRQSNCWT FROM IOSERVICEPROFILE WHERE EXISTS (SELECT /* InvOpt.skuDmdGrpEffIOPrmORSKUEffIOPrmSelectlivehint */ 1 FROM SKUEFFIOPARAM , PROCESSSKU WHERE SKUEFFIOPARAM.IOSERVICEPROFILE = IOSERVICEPROFILE.IOSERVICEPROFILE AND SKUEFFIOPARAM.ITEM = PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SKUEFFIOPARAM.LOC = PROCESSSKU.LOC AND PROCESSSKU.PROCESSID = :1 AND PROCESSSKU.BATCHNUM = :2 )

87fzyp4nvktqr.fullText: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LAST_HEARTBEAT = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) WHERE NODE_ID = :B2 AND ((LAST_HEARTBEAT IS NULL) OR ((SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - LAST_HEARTBEAT) > :B1 ) OR ((LAST_HEARTBEAT - SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP)) > :B1 ) )

8hqpjjsuv1vrb.fullText: SELECT c.schema_name, c.table_name, c.column_name, position FROM all_constraints uc, all_cons_columns ucc, md_column_info c, md_table_info t WHERE uc.status='ENABLED' AND c.is_configured=1 AND t.is_configured=1 AND uc.constraint_type='P' AND uc.constraint_name=ucc.constraint_name AND c.schema_name=t.schema_name AND c.schema_name=uc.owner AND c.schema_name=ucc.owner AND c.table_name=uc.table_name AND c.table_name=ucc.table_name AND c.table_name=t.table_name AND c.column_name = ucc.column_name ORDER BY uc.constraint_name

a635zr80ybnnw.fullText: SELECT /* RegularPlanShipSuppOrderTotalTVQHint */ planarriv.item, planarriv.source, planarriv.schedshipdate, planarriv.supporderqty FROM SKU, PLANARRIV WHERE ( PLANARRIV.ITEM=SKU.ITEM AND PLANARRIV.SOURCE=SKU.LOC) AND ( (sku.item, sku.loc) in ((select item, dest from sourcing where source = '029466-0' and dest = '3994' and transmode = 'TRUCK_CIGARET_3994' ) )) ORDER BY planarriv.item, planarriv.source, planarriv.schedshipdate ASC

aq0t5ju2h9tjh.fullText: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SRE_JOB, SRE_NODE_POOL_CONFIG WHERE SRE_JOB.SERVICE_NAME = SRE_NODE_POOL_CONFIG.SERVICE_NAME AND SRE_JOB.NODE_CONFIG_NAME = SRE_NODE_POOL_CONFIG.NODE_CONFIG_NAME AND SRE_NODE_POOL_CONFIG.POOL_NAME = :B4 AND ((JOB_TYPE = :B3 AND IS_CONTINUOUS_ENABLED = 1) OR (JOB_TYPE = :B2 ) ) AND JOB_STATE = :B1 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_ID IN (SELECT JOB_ID FROM (SELECT JOB_ID, ROWNUM JOBROWNUM FROM (SELECT JOB_ID FROM SRE_JOB WHERE ((((JOB_TYPE = :B3 AND IS_CONTINUOUS_ENABLED = 1) OR (JOB_TYPE = :B2 ) ) AND JOB_STATE = :B1 ) OR (JOB_ID = :B5 ) ) AND LOCKERS_NODE_ID IS NULL AND SUBMISSION_DATE <= SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) ORDER BY JOB_TYPE DESC, JOB_PRIORITY DESC, SUBMISSION_DATE, JOB_ID ) ) WHERE JOBROWNUM < (SELECT MIN(CANDIDATEROWNUM) + :B6 FROM (SELECT JOB_ID, ROWNUM CANDIDATEROWNUM FROM (SELECT JOB_ID FROM SRE_JOB WHERE ((((JOB_TYPE = : B3 AND IS_CONTINUOUS_ENABLED = 1) OR (JOB_TYPE = :B2 ) ) AND JOB_STATE = :B1 ) OR (JOB_ID = :B5 ) ) AND LOCKERS_NODE_ID IS NULL AND SUBMISSION_DATE <= SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) ORDER BY JOB_TYPE DESC, JOB_PRIORITY DESC, SUBMISSION_DATE, JOB_ID ) ) WHERE JOB_ID = :B5 ) )

bjp638q71rjbb.fullText: delete from LANEEXCEPTION /* InvOpt.RIO.PurgeLaneExceptionQueryLivehint */ WHERE ( EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM PROCESSLANE WHERE PROCESSLANE.source = LANEEXCEPTION.SOURCE AND PROCESSLANE.DEST = LANEEXCEPTION.DEST AND PROCESSLANE.TRANSMODE = LANEEXCEPTION.TRANSMODE AND processid = :1 AND batchnum = :2 AND ordergroup = ' ') OR (ordergroup IN (SELECT ordergroup FROM processlane WHERE processid = :3 AND BATCHNUM = :4 AND ordergroup <> ' '))) AND ( EXCEPTIONID >= 13000 AND EXCEPTIONID <= 13010 )

bzpgy1jz1q0fb.fullText: SELECT control.USERNAME, journal.MEASURE FROM SCPOMGR.MDAPJOURNALCONTROL control LEFT JOIN SCPOMGR.MDAPJOURNAL journal ON journal.COMMITID = control.COMMITID WHERE control.COMMITSTATE IN (2, 3, 4) ORDER BY control.USERNAME

d81xt4mfzs9qs.fullText: Select * from ordergroupmember where ordergroupmember in (SELECT ogm.ordergroupmember FROM ordergroupmember ogm LEFT JOIN sourcing s ON ogm.ordergroupmember = s.ordergroupmember WHERE s.ordergroupmember IS NULL AND ogm.ordergroupmember != ' ')

ddb1wkxqx3nur.fullText: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LOCKERS_NODE_ID = :B2 , STOP_REQUEST_DATE = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP), NODE_STATE = :B1 WHERE ROWNUM = 1 AND ((NODE_ID IN (SELECT SRE_NODE.NODE_ID FROM SRE_JOB, SRE_NODE, SRE_NODE_CONFIG WHERE SRE_NODE.NODE_ID = SRE_JOB.LOCKERS_NODE_ID AND SRE_NODE.NODE_STATE = :B4 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_STATE = :B3 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_ID = SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID AND SRE_JOB.NODE_CONFIG_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.NODE_CONFIG_NAME AND SRE_JOB.SERVICE_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.SERVICE_NAME AND SRE_NODE.NODE_ID <> :B2 AND SRE_NODE.LAST_HEARTBEAT < (SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - (NUMTODSINTERVAL(SRE_NODE_CONFIG.BLOCKING_INIT_TIMEOUT, 'SECOND')))) ) OR (NODE_ID IN (SELECT SRE_NODE.NODE_ID FROM SRE_JOB, SRE_NODE, SRE_NODE_CONFIG WHERE SRE_NODE.NODE_ID = SRE_JOB.LOCKERS_NODE_ID AND SRE_NODE.NODE_STATE = :B4 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_STATE = :B5 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_ID = SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID AND SRE_JOB.NODE_CONFIG_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.NODE_CONFIG_NAME AND SRE_JOB.SERVICE_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.SERVICE_NAME AND SRE_NODE.NODE_ID <> :B2 AND SRE_NODE.LAST_HEARTBEAT < (SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - (NUMTODSINTERVAL(SRE_NODE_CONFIG.NON_BLOCKING_INIT_TIMEOUT, 'SECOND')))) ) OR (NODE_ID IN (SELECT SRE_NODE.NODE_ID FROM SRE_JOB, SRE_NODE, SRE_NODE_CONFIG WHERE SRE_NODE.NODE_ID = SRE_JOB.LOCKERS_NODE_ID AND SRE_NODE.NODE_STATE = :B7 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_STATE = :B6 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_ID = SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID AND SRE_JOB.NODE_CONFIG_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.NODE_CONFIG_NAME AND SRE_JOB.SERVICE_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.SERVICE_NAME AND SRE_NODE.NODE_ID <> :B2 AND SRE_NODE.LAST_HEARTBEAT < (SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - (NUMTODSINTERVAL(SRE_NODE_CONFIG.BLOCKING_FINISH_TIMEOUT, 'SECOND')))) ) OR (NODE_ID IN (SELECT SRE_NODE.NODE_ID FROM SRE_JOB, SRE_NODE, SRE_NODE_CONFIG WHERE SRE_NODE.NODE_ID = SRE_JOB.LOCKERS_NODE_ID AND SRE_NODE.NODE_ST ATE = :B7 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_STATE = :B8 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_ID = SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID AND SRE_JOB.SERVICE_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.SERVICE_NAME AND SRE_JOB.NODE_CONFIG_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.NODE_CONFIG_NAME AND SRE_NODE.NODE_ID <> :B2 AND SRE_NODE.LAST_HEARTBEAT < (SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - (NUMTODSINTERVAL(SRE_NODE_CONFIG.CLOSE_JOB_TIMEOUT, 'SECOND')))) ) OR (NODE_ID IN (SELECT SRE_NODE.NODE_ID FROM SRE_NODE, SRE_JOB, SRE_NODE_CONFIG WHERE SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID = SRE_JOB.JOB_ID AND SRE_JOB.NODE_CONFIG_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.NODE_CONFIG_NAME AND SRE_JOB.SERVICE_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.SERVICE_NAME AND (SRE_JOB.LOCKERS_NODE_ID <> SRE_NODE.NODE_ID OR SRE_JOB.LOCKERS_NODE_ID IS NULL) AND SRE_NODE.LAST_HEARTBEAT < (SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - (NUMTODSINTERVAL(SRE_NODE_CONFIG.BUILD_ACTIONS_TIMEOUT, 'SECOND'))) AND SRE_NODE.NODE_STATE = :B4 AND SRE_NODE.NODE_ID <> :B2 ) ) OR (NODE_ID IN (SELECT SRE_NODE.NODE_ID FROM SRE_NODE, SRE_JOB, SRE_NODE_CONFIG WHERE SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID = SRE_JOB.JOB_ID AND SRE_JOB.NODE_CONFIG_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.NODE_CONFIG_NAME AND SRE_JOB.SERVICE_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.SERVICE_NAME AND (SRE_JOB.LOCKERS_NODE_ID <> SRE_NODE.NODE_ID OR SRE_JOB.LOCKERS_NODE_ID IS NULL) AND SRE_NODE.LAST_HEARTBEAT < (SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - (NUMTODSINTERVAL(SRE_NODE_CONFIG.RESET_TIMEOUT, 'SECOND'))) AND SRE_NODE.NODE_STATE = :B9 AND SRE_NODE.NODE_ID <> :B2 ) ) ) RETURNING CURRENT_JOB_ID, NODE_ID INTO :O0 , :O1

f3ww8rgva3hrs.fullText: update /* KSXM:FLUSH COL */ sys.col_usage$ set equality_preds = equality_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 1), 0, 0, 1), equijoin_preds = equijoin_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 2), 0, 0, 1), nonequijoin_preds = nonequijoin_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 4), 0, 0, 1), range_preds = range_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 8), 0, 0, 1), like_preds = like_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 16), 0, 0, 1), null_preds = null_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 32), 0, 0, 1), flags = flags + :flag - bitand(flags, :flag), timestamp = :time where obj# = :objn and intcol# = :coln

f5kwgwstvrj1k.fullText: SELECT /* RegularPlanShipSuppOrderTotalTVQHint */ planarriv.item, planarriv.source, planarriv.schedshipdate, planarriv.supporderqty FROM SKU, PLANARRIV WHERE ( PLANARRIV.ITEM=SKU.ITEM AND PLANARRIV.SOURCE=SKU.LOC) AND ( (sku.item, sku.loc) in ((select item, dest from sourcing where source = '412396-0' and dest = '3994' and transmode = 'TRUCK_412396-0_3994' ) )) ORDER BY planarriv.item, planarriv.source, planarriv.schedshipdate ASC

g71muy66ca7kq.fullText: SELECT /* OrderBuilderLocOnlineSrvcLoadByOrderGroupMemberHint */ l.loc, l.ohpost, l.workingcal, l.borrowingpct , 1 FROM Loc l WHERE l.loc in ( select source from Sourcing where orderGroup = :1 and orderGroupMember = :2 union select dest from Sourcing where orderGroup = :3 and ordergroupMember = :4 ) UNION SELECT /* OrderBuilderLocOnlineNonSrvcLoadByOrderGroupMemberHint */ UNIQUE l.loc, l.ohpost, l.workingcal, l.borrowingpct , 0 FROM Loc l WHERE l.loc in (select s.source from sourcing s, (select source, dest, transmode from sourcing where ordergroup= :5 and ordergroupmember=:6 ) s1 Where s1.dest=s.dest and s.ordergroup<> :7 and s.source<> s1.source )

_sql_text_map.8hqpjjsuv1vrb: SELECT c.schema_name, c.table_name, c.column_name, position FROM all_constraints uc, all_cons_columns ucc, md_column_info c, md_table_info t WHERE uc.status='ENABLED' AND c.is_configured=1 AND t.is_configured=1 AND uc.constraint_type='P' AND uc.constraint_name=ucc.constraint_name AND c.schema_name=t.schema_name AND c.schema_name=uc.owner AND c.schema_name=ucc.owner AND c.table_name=uc.table_name AND c.table_name=ucc.table_name AND c.table_name=t.table_name AND c.column_name = ucc.column_name ORDER BY uc.constraint_name

(NUMTODSINTERVAL(SRE_NODE_CONFIG.BLOCKING_FINISH_TIMEOUT, 'SECOND')))) ) OR (NODE_ID IN (SELECT SRE_NODE.NODE_ID FROM SRE_JOB, SRE_NODE, SRE_NODE_CONFIG WHERE SRE_NODE.NODE_ID = SRE_JOB.LOCKERS_NODE_ID AND SRE_NODE.NODE_ST ATE = :B7 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_STATE = :B8 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_ID = SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID AND SRE_JOB.SERVICE_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.SERVICE_NAME AND SRE_JOB.NODE_CONFIG_NAME = SRE_NODE_CONFIG.NODE_CONFIG_NAME AND SRE_NODE.NODE_ID <> :B2 AND SRE_NODE.LAST_HEARTBEAT < (SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - _sql_text_map.f3ww8rgva3hrs: update /* KSXM:FLUSH COL */ sys.col_usage$ set equality_preds = equality_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 1), 0, 0, 1), equijoin_preds = equijoin_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 2), 0, 0, 1), nonequijoin_preds = nonequijoin_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 4), 0, 0, 1), range_preds = range_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 8), 0, 0, 1), like_preds = like_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 16), 0, 0, 1), null_preds = null_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 32), 0, 0, 1), flags = flags + :flag - bitand(flags, :flag), timestamp = :time where obj# = :objn and intcol# = :coln
